sections.title: Mandatory Documents for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Successfully clearing a fastener shipment in Ghana depends entirely on the accuracy and completeness of your shipping dossier. Ghana Customs operates a digital, zero-tolerance documentation system. Discrepancies between your physical cargo and your paperwork will trigger immediate audits, leading to severe storage penalties.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                         MANDATORY DOCUMENT CHECKLIST                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [ ] Certificate of Conformity (CoC) - Issued via new GSA EasyPass Portal       |
|  [ ] Bill of Lading (B/L) - Express Release or Original Negotiable copies       |
|  [ ] Commercial Invoice - Itemized list of fasteners with exact HS Codes        |
|  [ ] Detailed Packing List - Net/gross weights, box counts, and heat numbers     |
|  [ ] Import Declaration Form (IDF) - Registered via ICUMS before shipping       |
|  [ ] Mill Test Certificates (MTC) - Certifying ISO 898-1 or ASTM mechanical properties|
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

The New GSA EasyPass Portal & Certificate of Conformity (CoC)

Effective July 1, 2026, the Ghana Standards Authority (GSA) assumed direct, exclusive control of the EasyPass PVOC Programme, ending all previous third-party service provider contracts. Under this updated framework, exporters must apply for and obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) directly through the new GSA EasyPass digital portal before shipping. Fasteners fall under restricted mechanical product categories; failing to upload an approved GSA CoC will block your customs clearance and trigger mandatory, costly local testing at Tema Port, alongside severe fines.  
VATupdate
+ 1

Essential Shipping and Customs Documentation

Beyond the GSA certificate, your clearance package must include:

Commercial Invoice: This must show an itemized breakdown of the fastener order. Include separate lines for bolts, nuts, and washers to prevent valuation disputes. It must state the exact unit price, total Free on Board (FOB) value, freight costs, and insurance details.

Packing List: This document must detail the specific contents of each wooden crate or pallet. It should clearly list the total net weight, gross weight, packaging types (e.g., standard 25 kg bags inside outer cartons), and the exact quantity per SKU.

Bill of Lading (B/L): Issued by the ocean carrier, this must match the commercial invoice exactly regarding the consignee's name, Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Ghana Card details, and container seal numbers.

Import Declaration Form (IDF): This is filed electronically through the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS) prior to the ship’s departure from the port of loading.  
Ascope Shipping

Mill Test Certificates (MTC): Crucial for industrial bulk orders, these documents must be supplied by the manufacturer (e.g., TradeGo Fasteners) to verify chemical compositions and mechanical grades, such as Class 8.8, Class 10.9, or ASTM A325 specifications.

sections.title: Customs Clearance Steps for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Ghana Customs utilizes the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS), a unified digital platform that has automated and streamlined cargo processing at Tema Port. Clearing industrial hardware shipments involves three distinct phases.  
Ascope Shipping

       +----------------------------------------------------------+
       | PRE-ARRIVAL PHASE                                        |
       | • Register IDF in ICUMS                                  |
       | • Obtain GSA CoC via EasyPass Portal                     |
       | • Shipping Line uploads Cargo Manifest                   |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | ON-SITE & ASSESSMENT PHASE                               |
       | • Submit entry via Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)     |
       | • ICUMS issues CCVR (Value & Tariff Verification)        |
       | • Pay Duties through Ghana.gov or partner banks           |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | INSPECTION & RELEASE PHASE                               |
       | • Channel Assignment (Green, Yellow, or Red)             |
       | • Physical Inspection / GSA Verification (if Red/Yellow) |
       | • Settle Port/Terminal fees & Gate-Out Cargo             |
       +----------------------------------------------------------+

1. Pre-Arrival Document Submission

The clearance process begins well before the container vessel berths at Tema Port. Once TradeGo Fasteners loads your container and provides the final Bill of Lading, your licensed Customs House Agent (CHA) uploads the commercial invoice, packing list, IDF, and GSA CoC to the ICUMS portal. Simultaneously, the shipping line submits the electronic cargo manifest to the GRA Customs system.  
Guazi
+ 1

ICUMS uses a centralized Home Delivery Value (HDV) database to automatically verify your declared transaction values against global steel averages and historical import records. If your invoice pricing aligns with these market indices, the system smoothly generates a Customs Classification and Valuation Report (CCVR), which specifies the exact duties and taxes owed.  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

2. Physical Cargo Inspection and Channel Routing

Upon the vessel’s arrival and container discharge at the Tema Port terminal, the cargo is routed through one of three ICUMS customs channels:

Green Channel (Direct Release): Paperwork and compliance history are perfect. The cargo is cleared without physical inspection.

Yellow Channel (Document Audit): Discrepancies in HS classifications or missing GSA paperwork trigger an in-depth document review.

Red Channel (Physical Examination): Mandatory for first-time importers, high-volume construction contracts, or shipments flagged with valuation concerns.

During a Red Channel physical examination, customs officers and GSA inspectors verify that the physical fasteners match the declared steel grades, thread types, and dimensions. They will check outer packaging labels for the manufacturer's mark, grade markings (e.g., "8.8" or "A325" stamped on bolt heads), and trace them back to the GSA CoC and Mill Test Certificates.  
Guazi

3. Final Duty Payment and Cargo Release

Once inspection officers clear the shipment, the final step is paying the calculated duties and levies through the Ghana.gov payment portal or authorized partner banks. After payment, the shipping agent issues a Delivery Order (DO), and the Tema Port cargo handling terminal releases the container for transport.  
Ascope Shipping

sections.title: Tariff and Tax Rules for Ghana Fastener Imports at Tema Port
sections.body:
For B2B buyers in Ghana, accurately projecting the Total Landed Cost of fasteners is vital to maintaining project margins. Ghana uses the standardized Harmonized System (HS) Code Chapter 7318 to classify iron and steel fasteners.  
Flexport

Harmonized System (HS) Classifications for Fasteners
HS Code	Product Description	Standard Import Duty
7318.15	Threaded bolts, screws, and studs (with/without nuts/washers)	20%
7318.16	Threaded nuts (hex, lock, flange, heavy hex)	20%
7318.21	Spring washers and other lock washers	20%
7318.22	Non-threaded flat washers	20%
7318.14	Self-tapping screws	20%
Detailed Breakdown of Taxes, Levies, and Fees

Import duties are calculated on the CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) value of the shipment. In addition to the base 20% import duty, several local taxes, statutory levies, and administrative fees are applied during the ICUMS processing phase:  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

Value Added Tax (VAT): 12.5% (calculated on the CIF value plus the import duty).

National Health Insurance Levy (NHIL): 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Education Trust Fund (GETFund) Levy: 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy: 1.0%.

Special Import Levy (SIL): 2.0%.  
Ascope Shipping

ECOWAS Levy: 0.5% (applicable to non-ECOWAS originating cargo).

African Union (AU) Import Levy: 0.2%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Export-Import (EXIM) Bank Levy: 0.75%.  
Ascope Shipping

AfCFTA Preferential Trade Note: Under the rules of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), B2B buyers importing fasteners from other qualified African manufacturing hubs can access significantly reduced or eliminated import tariffs. However, since the bulk of heavy-duty, high-tensile industrial fasteners are still imported from specialized Chinese manufacturers, the standard 20% external tariff and associated levies remain the primary cost structure for most shipments arriving at Tema Port.

Tax Exemptions and Special Incentives

Ghana offers valuable customs exemptions for large-scale industrial projects. Under the Ghana Investment Promotion Centre (GIPC) framework, registered companies executing strategic national infrastructure, mining, or agricultural projects can apply for duty-and-VAT exemptions on bulk imports of industrial fasteners. To benefit from these concessions, the importer must obtain a formal exemption letter from the Ministry of Finance before registering the cargo's IDF in ICUMS.

sections.title: Common Delays and Solutions for Tema Port Fastener Shipment
sections.body:
Even minor errors can stall a Tema port cargo clearance process, transforming an efficient logistics plan into a costly headache. Port demurrage—the daily penalty charged by shipping lines for containers left past their free-time allowance—can quickly erode your profits.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                            TEMA PORT DELAY POINTS & B2B SOLUTIONS                                 |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  1. ICUMS Valuation Flag                                                                          |
|     * Cause: Artificially low invoice values triggered by automatic database checks.              |
|     * Solution: TradeGo Fasteners provides authentic proof of payment and wire transfers.         |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  2. GSA EasyPass Non-Compliance                                                                   |
|     * Cause: Missing or expired Certificate of Conformity (CoC) at arrival.                       |
|     * Solution: Generate the CoC via the GSA digital portal prior to container loading.           |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  3. Weight and Packing Mismatches                                                                 |
|     * Cause: Discrepancies between the packing list and physical container scale weight.          |
|     * Solution: Implement rigorous double-weighing protocols at the factory before sealing.        |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  4. Inconsistent Steel Grade Labeling                                                             |
|     * Cause: Physical markings on fasteners don't match the grades on test certificates.          |
|     * Solution: Standardize laser-etched markings (8.8, 10.9, A325) to align with ISO/ASTM norms. |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Mitigating ICUMS Valuation Challenges

The GRA’s automated valuation system is highly sensitive to price variations. If your invoice values are lower than the ICUMS benchmark for carbon steel, the system will flag your shipment for undervaluation. To resolve this quickly, always keep a complete "valuation defense package" ready. This should include your signed purchase contract, proof of the advanced bank transfer (such as an escrow receipt or bank swift slip), and a detailed manufacturer price list that explains any volume discounts.  
West Coast Shipping

Navigating Container Handling Fees and Local Port Costs

To protect businesses from unpredictable shipping agent fees, the Ghana Shippers' Authority (GSA) secured a key legal ruling in July 2026. This decision allows the GSA to enforce a strict regulatory cap on local container handling and administrative charges billed by shipping lines. Importers should carefully review local terminal invoices; if a shipping line's local agent charges more than the GSA-mandated ceiling, you can file an immediate complaint with the GSA compliance task force to resolve the billing dispute.  
GBC Ghana Online

sections.title: B2B Fastener Import Best Practices for Tema Port Ghana
sections.body:
Successfully managing imports to Tema Port requires close cooperation between the industrial purchaser, a reliable Chinese manufacturer, and an experienced local logistics partner.

1. Optimize Shipping and Container Loading

Fasteners are high-density, high-weight cargo. A single 20-foot GP container can easily reach its maximum weight limit (typically 26 to 28 metric tons) while remaining only half-full by volume.

Select 20-foot Containers: Always use 20-foot containers rather than 40-foot units for heavy cargo like fasteners. This optimizes ocean freight costs and keeps transport weights within legal limits.

Reinforced Packing: Require your manufacturer to use heavy-duty, moisture-resistant, ISPM-15 certified wooden pallets or crates. Double-strap each pallet to prevent shifting during transit, which can damage packaging and lead to safety rejections during Tema customs inspections.

2. Partner with a Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)

Do not simply hire the cheapest clearing agent. Partner with a reputable, licensed customs broker who has a proven track record of handling heavy metals and industrial hardware. An experienced agent will proactively review your documentation, track your vessel's arrival, and resolve any ICUMS valuation or GSA EasyPass queries within your port's free-time window.  
Guazi

3. Establish a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

For recurring projects, establish a standard compliance checklist with your Chinese supplier. This ensures that every shipment uses consistent HS codes, matches GSA quality standards, and includes accurate packing lists. This repeatable approach minimizes compliance risks, speeds up your clearances, and keeps your supply chain running smoothly.

sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.body:
### What is the core Ghana Tema Port fastener import procedure?
The core procedure involves registering an Import Declaration Form (IDF) in ICUMS, obtaining a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) via the GSA EasyPass portal, paying assessed duties, undergoing customs inspection at Tema Port, and settling port terminal handling fees before final gate-out.  
VATupdate

### What documents are needed for fastener import at Tema Port?
You must provide a commercial invoice, a detailed packing list, a Bill of Lading, an Import Declaration Form (IDF), a GSA Certificate of Conformity (CoC), and Mill Test Certificates (MTC) to verify the fasteners' technical and chemical properties.

### How long does Tema Port fastener customs clearance take?
With complete, compliant documentation and pre-funded customs duties, standard clearance at Tema Port takes between 3 to 7 working days. If you run into documentation errors, valuation disputes, or GSA EasyPass issues, clearances can easily stretch to 2 or 3 weeks.  
Guazi
+ 1

### Are there special tariffs for bulk fastener imports to Ghana?
The standard import duty for fasteners under HS Chapter 7318 is 20%, calculated on a CIF basis. However, B2B importers working on large-scale national infrastructure, mining, or agricultural projects can apply for duty and tax exemptions through GIPC and the Ministry of Finance.

### How to avoid import delays for fasteners at Tema Port?
To prevent delays, ensure your supplier obtains the GSA Certificate of Conformity via the EasyPass portal before shipping. Additionally, verify that all invoice values align with current steel market rates, use precise packing lists, and pre-fund all customs duties through the Ghana.gov platform.  
VATupdate
+ 1

sections.title: Mandatory Documents for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Successfully clearing a fastener shipment in Ghana depends entirely on the accuracy and completeness of your shipping dossier. Ghana Customs operates a digital, zero-tolerance documentation system. Discrepancies between your physical cargo and your paperwork will trigger immediate audits, leading to severe storage penalties.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                         MANDATORY DOCUMENT CHECKLIST                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [ ] Certificate of Conformity (CoC) - Issued via new GSA EasyPass Portal       |
|  [ ] Bill of Lading (B/L) - Express Release or Original Negotiable copies       |
|  [ ] Commercial Invoice - Itemized list of fasteners with exact HS Codes        |
|  [ ] Detailed Packing List - Net/gross weights, box counts, and heat numbers     |
|  [ ] Import Declaration Form (IDF) - Registered via ICUMS before shipping       |
|  [ ] Mill Test Certificates (MTC) - Certifying ISO 898-1 or ASTM mechanical properties|
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

The New GSA EasyPass Portal & Certificate of Conformity (CoC)

Effective July 1, 2026, the Ghana Standards Authority (GSA) assumed direct, exclusive control of the EasyPass PVOC Programme, ending all previous third-party service provider contracts. Under this updated framework, exporters must apply for and obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) directly through the new GSA EasyPass digital portal before shipping. Fasteners fall under restricted mechanical product categories; failing to upload an approved GSA CoC will block your customs clearance and trigger mandatory, costly local testing at Tema Port, alongside severe fines.  
VATupdate
+ 1

Essential Shipping and Customs Documentation

Beyond the GSA certificate, your clearance package must include:

Commercial Invoice: This must show an itemized breakdown of the fastener order. Include separate lines for bolts, nuts, and washers to prevent valuation disputes. It must state the exact unit price, total Free on Board (FOB) value, freight costs, and insurance details.

Packing List: This document must detail the specific contents of each wooden crate or pallet. It should clearly list the total net weight, gross weight, packaging types (e.g., standard 25 kg bags inside outer cartons), and the exact quantity per SKU.

Bill of Lading (B/L): Issued by the ocean carrier, this must match the commercial invoice exactly regarding the consignee's name, Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Ghana Card details, and container seal numbers.

Import Declaration Form (IDF): This is filed electronically through the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS) prior to the ship’s departure from the port of loading.  
Ascope Shipping

Mill Test Certificates (MTC): Crucial for industrial bulk orders, these documents must be supplied by the manufacturer (e.g., TradeGo Fasteners) to verify chemical compositions and mechanical grades, such as Class 8.8, Class 10.9, or ASTM A325 specifications.

sections.title: Customs Clearance Steps for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Ghana Customs utilizes the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS), a unified digital platform that has automated and streamlined cargo processing at Tema Port. Clearing industrial hardware shipments involves three distinct phases.  
Ascope Shipping

       +----------------------------------------------------------+
       | PRE-ARRIVAL PHASE                                        |
       | • Register IDF in ICUMS                                  |
       | • Obtain GSA CoC via EasyPass Portal                     |
       | • Shipping Line uploads Cargo Manifest                   |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | ON-SITE & ASSESSMENT PHASE                               |
       | • Submit entry via Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)     |
       | • ICUMS issues CCVR (Value & Tariff Verification)        |
       | • Pay Duties through Ghana.gov or partner banks           |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | INSPECTION & RELEASE PHASE                               |
       | • Channel Assignment (Green, Yellow, or Red)             |
       | • Physical Inspection / GSA Verification (if Red/Yellow) |
       | • Settle Port/Terminal fees & Gate-Out Cargo             |
       +----------------------------------------------------------+

1. Pre-Arrival Document Submission

The clearance process begins well before the container vessel berths at Tema Port. Once TradeGo Fasteners loads your container and provides the final Bill of Lading, your licensed Customs House Agent (CHA) uploads the commercial invoice, packing list, IDF, and GSA CoC to the ICUMS portal. Simultaneously, the shipping line submits the electronic cargo manifest to the GRA Customs system.  
Guazi
+ 1

ICUMS uses a centralized Home Delivery Value (HDV) database to automatically verify your declared transaction values against global steel averages and historical import records. If your invoice pricing aligns with these market indices, the system smoothly generates a Customs Classification and Valuation Report (CCVR), which specifies the exact duties and taxes owed.  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

2. Physical Cargo Inspection and Channel Routing

Upon the vessel’s arrival and container discharge at the Tema Port terminal, the cargo is routed through one of three ICUMS customs channels:

Green Channel (Direct Release): Paperwork and compliance history are perfect. The cargo is cleared without physical inspection.

Yellow Channel (Document Audit): Discrepancies in HS classifications or missing GSA paperwork trigger an in-depth document review.

Red Channel (Physical Examination): Mandatory for first-time importers, high-volume construction contracts, or shipments flagged with valuation concerns.

During a Red Channel physical examination, customs officers and GSA inspectors verify that the physical fasteners match the declared steel grades, thread types, and dimensions. They will check outer packaging labels for the manufacturer's mark, grade markings (e.g., "8.8" or "A325" stamped on bolt heads), and trace them back to the GSA CoC and Mill Test Certificates.  
Guazi

3. Final Duty Payment and Cargo Release

Once inspection officers clear the shipment, the final step is paying the calculated duties and levies through the Ghana.gov payment portal or authorized partner banks. After payment, the shipping agent issues a Delivery Order (DO), and the Tema Port cargo handling terminal releases the container for transport.  
Ascope Shipping

sections.title: Tariff and Tax Rules for Ghana Fastener Imports at Tema Port
sections.body:
For B2B buyers in Ghana, accurately projecting the Total Landed Cost of fasteners is vital to maintaining project margins. Ghana uses the standardized Harmonized System (HS) Code Chapter 7318 to classify iron and steel fasteners.  
Flexport

Harmonized System (HS) Classifications for Fasteners
HS Code	Product Description	Standard Import Duty
7318.15	Threaded bolts, screws, and studs (with/without nuts/washers)	20%
7318.16	Threaded nuts (hex, lock, flange, heavy hex)	20%
7318.21	Spring washers and other lock washers	20%
7318.22	Non-threaded flat washers	20%
7318.14	Self-tapping screws	20%
Detailed Breakdown of Taxes, Levies, and Fees

Import duties are calculated on the CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) value of the shipment. In addition to the base 20% import duty, several local taxes, statutory levies, and administrative fees are applied during the ICUMS processing phase:  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

Value Added Tax (VAT): 12.5% (calculated on the CIF value plus the import duty).

National Health Insurance Levy (NHIL): 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Education Trust Fund (GETFund) Levy: 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy: 1.0%.

Special Import Levy (SIL): 2.0%.  
Ascope Shipping

ECOWAS Levy: 0.5% (applicable to non-ECOWAS originating cargo).

African Union (AU) Import Levy: 0.2%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Export-Import (EXIM) Bank Levy: 0.75%.  
Ascope Shipping

AfCFTA Preferential Trade Note: Under the rules of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), B2B buyers importing fasteners from other qualified African manufacturing hubs can access significantly reduced or eliminated import tariffs. However, since the bulk of heavy-duty, high-tensile industrial fasteners are still imported from specialized Chinese manufacturers, the standard 20% external tariff and associated levies remain the primary cost structure for most shipments arriving at Tema Port.

Tax Exemptions and Special Incentives

Ghana offers valuable customs exemptions for large-scale industrial projects. Under the Ghana Investment Promotion Centre (GIPC) framework, registered companies executing strategic national infrastructure, mining, or agricultural projects can apply for duty-and-VAT exemptions on bulk imports of industrial fasteners. To benefit from these concessions, the importer must obtain a formal exemption letter from the Ministry of Finance before registering the cargo's IDF in ICUMS.

sections.title: Common Delays and Solutions for Tema Port Fastener Shipment
sections.body:
Even minor errors can stall a Tema port cargo clearance process, transforming an efficient logistics plan into a costly headache. Port demurrage—the daily penalty charged by shipping lines for containers left past their free-time allowance—can quickly erode your profits.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                            TEMA PORT DELAY POINTS & B2B SOLUTIONS                                 |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  1. ICUMS Valuation Flag                                                                          |
|     * Cause: Artificially low invoice values triggered by automatic database checks.              |
|     * Solution: TradeGo Fasteners provides authentic proof of payment and wire transfers.         |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  2. GSA EasyPass Non-Compliance                                                                   |
|     * Cause: Missing or expired Certificate of Conformity (CoC) at arrival.                       |
|     * Solution: Generate the CoC via the GSA digital portal prior to container loading.           |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  3. Weight and Packing Mismatches                                                                 |
|     * Cause: Discrepancies between the packing list and physical container scale weight.          |
|     * Solution: Implement rigorous double-weighing protocols at the factory before sealing.        |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  4. Inconsistent Steel Grade Labeling                                                             |
|     * Cause: Physical markings on fasteners don't match the grades on test certificates.          |
|     * Solution: Standardize laser-etched markings (8.8, 10.9, A325) to align with ISO/ASTM norms. |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Mitigating ICUMS Valuation Challenges

The GRA’s automated valuation system is highly sensitive to price variations. If your invoice values are lower than the ICUMS benchmark for carbon steel, the system will flag your shipment for undervaluation. To resolve this quickly, always keep a complete "valuation defense package" ready. This should include your signed purchase contract, proof of the advanced bank transfer (such as an escrow receipt or bank swift slip), and a detailed manufacturer price list that explains any volume discounts.  
West Coast Shipping

Navigating Container Handling Fees and Local Port Costs

To protect businesses from unpredictable shipping agent fees, the Ghana Shippers' Authority (GSA) secured a key legal ruling in July 2026. This decision allows the GSA to enforce a strict regulatory cap on local container handling and administrative charges billed by shipping lines. Importers should carefully review local terminal invoices; if a shipping line's local agent charges more than the GSA-mandated ceiling, you can file an immediate complaint with the GSA compliance task force to resolve the billing dispute.  
GBC Ghana Online

sections.title: B2B Fastener Import Best Practices for Tema Port Ghana
sections.body:
Successfully managing imports to Tema Port requires close cooperation between the industrial purchaser, a reliable Chinese manufacturer, and an experienced local logistics partner.

1. Optimize Shipping and Container Loading

Fasteners are high-density, high-weight cargo. A single 20-foot GP container can easily reach its maximum weight limit (typically 26 to 28 metric tons) while remaining only half-full by volume.

Select 20-foot Containers: Always use 20-foot containers rather than 40-foot units for heavy cargo like fasteners. This optimizes ocean freight costs and keeps transport weights within legal limits.

Reinforced Packing: Require your manufacturer to use heavy-duty, moisture-resistant, ISPM-15 certified wooden pallets or crates. Double-strap each pallet to prevent shifting during transit, which can damage packaging and lead to safety rejections during Tema customs inspections.

2. Partner with a Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)

Do not simply hire the cheapest clearing agent. Partner with a reputable, licensed customs broker who has a proven track record of handling heavy metals and industrial hardware. An experienced agent will proactively review your documentation, track your vessel's arrival, and resolve any ICUMS valuation or GSA EasyPass queries within your port's free-time window.  
Guazi

3. Establish a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

For recurring projects, establish a standard compliance checklist with your Chinese supplier. This ensures that every shipment uses consistent HS codes, matches GSA quality standards, and includes accurate packing lists. This repeatable approach minimizes compliance risks, speeds up your clearances, and keeps your supply chain running smoothly.

With complete, compliant documentation and pre-funded customs duties, standard clearance at Tema Port takes between 3 to 7 working days. If you run into documentation errors, valuation disputes, or GSA EasyPass issues, clearances can easily stretch to 2 or 3 weeks.

To prevent delays, ensure your supplier obtains the GSA Certificate of Conformity via the EasyPass portal before shipping. Additionally, verify that all invoice values align with current steel market rates, use precise packing lists, and pre-fund all customs duties through the Ghana.gov platform.

The core procedure involves registering an Import Declaration Form (IDF) in ICUMS, obtaining a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) via the GSA EasyPass portal, paying assessed duties, undergoing customs inspection at Tema Port, and settling port terminal handling fees before final gate-out.  
VATupdate

You must provide a commercial invoice, a detailed packing list, a Bill of Lading, an Import Declaration Form (IDF), a GSA Certificate of Conformity (CoC), and Mill Test Certificates (MTC) to verify the fasteners' technical and chemical properties.

### How long does Tema Port fastener customs clearance take?
With complete, compliant documentation and pre-funded customs duties, standard clearance at Tema Port takes between 3 to 7 working days. If you run into documentation errors, valuation disputes, or GSA EasyPass issues, clearances can easily stretch to 2 or 3 weeks.  
Guazi

### How to avoid import delays for fasteners at Tema Port?
To prevent delays, ensure your supplier obtains the GSA Certificate of Conformity via the EasyPass portal before shipping. Additionally, verify that all invoice values align with current steel market rates, use precise packing lists, and pre-fund all customs duties through the Ghana.gov platform.

sections.title: Mandatory Documents for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Successfully clearing a fastener shipment in Ghana depends entirely on the accuracy and completeness of your shipping dossier. Ghana Customs operates a digital, zero-tolerance documentation system. Discrepancies between your physical cargo and your paperwork will trigger immediate audits, leading to severe storage penalties.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                         MANDATORY DOCUMENT CHECKLIST                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [ ] Certificate of Conformity (CoC) - Issued via new GSA EasyPass Portal       |
|  [ ] Bill of Lading (B/L) - Express Release or Original Negotiable copies       |
|  [ ] Commercial Invoice - Itemized list of fasteners with exact HS Codes        |
|  [ ] Detailed Packing List - Net/gross weights, box counts, and heat numbers     |
|  [ ] Import Declaration Form (IDF) - Registered via ICUMS before shipping       |
|  [ ] Mill Test Certificates (MTC) - Certifying ISO 898-1 or ASTM mechanical properties|
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

The New GSA EasyPass Portal & Certificate of Conformity (CoC)

Effective July 1, 2026, the Ghana Standards Authority (GSA) assumed direct, exclusive control of the EasyPass PVOC Programme, ending all previous third-party service provider contracts. Under this updated framework, exporters must apply for and obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) directly through the new GSA EasyPass digital portal before shipping. Fasteners fall under restricted mechanical product categories; failing to upload an approved GSA CoC will block your customs clearance and trigger mandatory, costly local testing at Tema Port, alongside severe fines.  
VATupdate
+ 1

Essential Shipping and Customs Documentation

Beyond the GSA certificate, your clearance package must include:

Commercial Invoice: This must show an itemized breakdown of the fastener order. Include separate lines for bolts, nuts, and washers to prevent valuation disputes. It must state the exact unit price, total Free on Board (FOB) value, freight costs, and insurance details.

Packing List: This document must detail the specific contents of each wooden crate or pallet. It should clearly list the total net weight, gross weight, packaging types (e.g., standard 25 kg bags inside outer cartons), and the exact quantity per SKU.

Bill of Lading (B/L): Issued by the ocean carrier, this must match the commercial invoice exactly regarding the consignee's name, Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Ghana Card details, and container seal numbers.

Import Declaration Form (IDF): This is filed electronically through the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS) prior to the ship’s departure from the port of loading.  
Ascope Shipping

Mill Test Certificates (MTC): Crucial for industrial bulk orders, these documents must be supplied by the manufacturer (e.g., TradeGo Fasteners) to verify chemical compositions and mechanical grades, such as Class 8.8, Class 10.9, or ASTM A325 specifications.

sections.title: Customs Clearance Steps for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Ghana Customs utilizes the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS), a unified digital platform that has automated and streamlined cargo processing at Tema Port. Clearing industrial hardware shipments involves three distinct phases.  
Ascope Shipping

       +----------------------------------------------------------+
       | PRE-ARRIVAL PHASE                                        |
       | • Register IDF in ICUMS                                  |
       | • Obtain GSA CoC via EasyPass Portal                     |
       | • Shipping Line uploads Cargo Manifest                   |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | ON-SITE & ASSESSMENT PHASE                               |
       | • Submit entry via Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)     |
       | • ICUMS issues CCVR (Value & Tariff Verification)        |
       | • Pay Duties through Ghana.gov or partner banks           |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | INSPECTION & RELEASE PHASE                               |
       | • Channel Assignment (Green, Yellow, or Red)             |
       | • Physical Inspection / GSA Verification (if Red/Yellow) |
       | • Settle Port/Terminal fees & Gate-Out Cargo             |
       +----------------------------------------------------------+

1. Pre-Arrival Document Submission

The clearance process begins well before the container vessel berths at Tema Port. Once TradeGo Fasteners loads your container and provides the final Bill of Lading, your licensed Customs House Agent (CHA) uploads the commercial invoice, packing list, IDF, and GSA CoC to the ICUMS portal. Simultaneously, the shipping line submits the electronic cargo manifest to the GRA Customs system.  
Guazi
+ 1

ICUMS uses a centralized Home Delivery Value (HDV) database to automatically verify your declared transaction values against global steel averages and historical import records. If your invoice pricing aligns with these market indices, the system smoothly generates a Customs Classification and Valuation Report (CCVR), which specifies the exact duties and taxes owed.  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

2. Physical Cargo Inspection and Channel Routing

Upon the vessel’s arrival and container discharge at the Tema Port terminal, the cargo is routed through one of three ICUMS customs channels:

Green Channel (Direct Release): Paperwork and compliance history are perfect. The cargo is cleared without physical inspection.

Yellow Channel (Document Audit): Discrepancies in HS classifications or missing GSA paperwork trigger an in-depth document review.

Red Channel (Physical Examination): Mandatory for first-time importers, high-volume construction contracts, or shipments flagged with valuation concerns.

During a Red Channel physical examination, customs officers and GSA inspectors verify that the physical fasteners match the declared steel grades, thread types, and dimensions. They will check outer packaging labels for the manufacturer's mark, grade markings (e.g., "8.8" or "A325" stamped on bolt heads), and trace them back to the GSA CoC and Mill Test Certificates.  
Guazi

3. Final Duty Payment and Cargo Release

Once inspection officers clear the shipment, the final step is paying the calculated duties and levies through the Ghana.gov payment portal or authorized partner banks. After payment, the shipping agent issues a Delivery Order (DO), and the Tema Port cargo handling terminal releases the container for transport.  
Ascope Shipping

sections.title: Tariff and Tax Rules for Ghana Fastener Imports at Tema Port
sections.body:
For B2B buyers in Ghana, accurately projecting the Total Landed Cost of fasteners is vital to maintaining project margins. Ghana uses the standardized Harmonized System (HS) Code Chapter 7318 to classify iron and steel fasteners.  
Flexport

Harmonized System (HS) Classifications for Fasteners
HS Code	Product Description	Standard Import Duty
7318.15	Threaded bolts, screws, and studs (with/without nuts/washers)	20%
7318.16	Threaded nuts (hex, lock, flange, heavy hex)	20%
7318.21	Spring washers and other lock washers	20%
7318.22	Non-threaded flat washers	20%
7318.14	Self-tapping screws	20%
Detailed Breakdown of Taxes, Levies, and Fees

Import duties are calculated on the CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) value of the shipment. In addition to the base 20% import duty, several local taxes, statutory levies, and administrative fees are applied during the ICUMS processing phase:  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

Value Added Tax (VAT): 12.5% (calculated on the CIF value plus the import duty).

National Health Insurance Levy (NHIL): 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Education Trust Fund (GETFund) Levy: 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy: 1.0%.

Special Import Levy (SIL): 2.0%.  
Ascope Shipping

ECOWAS Levy: 0.5% (applicable to non-ECOWAS originating cargo).

African Union (AU) Import Levy: 0.2%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Export-Import (EXIM) Bank Levy: 0.75%.  
Ascope Shipping

AfCFTA Preferential Trade Note: Under the rules of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), B2B buyers importing fasteners from other qualified African manufacturing hubs can access significantly reduced or eliminated import tariffs. However, since the bulk of heavy-duty, high-tensile industrial fasteners are still imported from specialized Chinese manufacturers, the standard 20% external tariff and associated levies remain the primary cost structure for most shipments arriving at Tema Port.

Tax Exemptions and Special Incentives

Ghana offers valuable customs exemptions for large-scale industrial projects. Under the Ghana Investment Promotion Centre (GIPC) framework, registered companies executing strategic national infrastructure, mining, or agricultural projects can apply for duty-and-VAT exemptions on bulk imports of industrial fasteners. To benefit from these concessions, the importer must obtain a formal exemption letter from the Ministry of Finance before registering the cargo's IDF in ICUMS.

sections.title: Common Delays and Solutions for Tema Port Fastener Shipment
sections.body:
Even minor errors can stall a Tema port cargo clearance process, transforming an efficient logistics plan into a costly headache. Port demurrage—the daily penalty charged by shipping lines for containers left past their free-time allowance—can quickly erode your profits.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                            TEMA PORT DELAY POINTS & B2B SOLUTIONS                                 |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  1. ICUMS Valuation Flag                                                                          |
|     * Cause: Artificially low invoice values triggered by automatic database checks.              |
|     * Solution: TradeGo Fasteners provides authentic proof of payment and wire transfers.         |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  2. GSA EasyPass Non-Compliance                                                                   |
|     * Cause: Missing or expired Certificate of Conformity (CoC) at arrival.                       |
|     * Solution: Generate the CoC via the GSA digital portal prior to container loading.           |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  3. Weight and Packing Mismatches                                                                 |
|     * Cause: Discrepancies between the packing list and physical container scale weight.          |
|     * Solution: Implement rigorous double-weighing protocols at the factory before sealing.        |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  4. Inconsistent Steel Grade Labeling                                                             |
|     * Cause: Physical markings on fasteners don't match the grades on test certificates.          |
|     * Solution: Standardize laser-etched markings (8.8, 10.9, A325) to align with ISO/ASTM norms. |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Mitigating ICUMS Valuation Challenges

The GRA’s automated valuation system is highly sensitive to price variations. If your invoice values are lower than the ICUMS benchmark for carbon steel, the system will flag your shipment for undervaluation. To resolve this quickly, always keep a complete "valuation defense package" ready. This should include your signed purchase contract, proof of the advanced bank transfer (such as an escrow receipt or bank swift slip), and a detailed manufacturer price list that explains any volume discounts.  
West Coast Shipping

Navigating Container Handling Fees and Local Port Costs

To protect businesses from unpredictable shipping agent fees, the Ghana Shippers' Authority (GSA) secured a key legal ruling in July 2026. This decision allows the GSA to enforce a strict regulatory cap on local container handling and administrative charges billed by shipping lines. Importers should carefully review local terminal invoices; if a shipping line's local agent charges more than the GSA-mandated ceiling, you can file an immediate complaint with the GSA compliance task force to resolve the billing dispute.  
GBC Ghana Online

sections.title: B2B Fastener Import Best Practices for Tema Port Ghana
sections.body:
Successfully managing imports to Tema Port requires close cooperation between the industrial purchaser, a reliable Chinese manufacturer, and an experienced local logistics partner.

1. Optimize Shipping and Container Loading

Fasteners are high-density, high-weight cargo. A single 20-foot GP container can easily reach its maximum weight limit (typically 26 to 28 metric tons) while remaining only half-full by volume.

Select 20-foot Containers: Always use 20-foot containers rather than 40-foot units for heavy cargo like fasteners. This optimizes ocean freight costs and keeps transport weights within legal limits.

Reinforced Packing: Require your manufacturer to use heavy-duty, moisture-resistant, ISPM-15 certified wooden pallets or crates. Double-strap each pallet to prevent shifting during transit, which can damage packaging and lead to safety rejections during Tema customs inspections.

2. Partner with a Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)

Do not simply hire the cheapest clearing agent. Partner with a reputable, licensed customs broker who has a proven track record of handling heavy metals and industrial hardware. An experienced agent will proactively review your documentation, track your vessel's arrival, and resolve any ICUMS valuation or GSA EasyPass queries within your port's free-time window.  
Guazi

3. Establish a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

For recurring projects, establish a standard compliance checklist with your Chinese supplier. This ensures that every shipment uses consistent HS codes, matches GSA quality standards, and includes accurate packing lists. This repeatable approach minimizes compliance risks, speeds up your clearances, and keeps your supply chain running smoothly.

sections.body:
Successfully clearing a fastener shipment in Ghana depends entirely on the accuracy and completeness of your shipping dossier. Ghana Customs operates a digital, zero-tolerance documentation system. +---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                         MANDATORY DOCUMENT CHECKLIST                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [ ] Certificate of Conformity (CoC) - Issued via new GSA EasyPass Portal       |
|  [ ] Bill of Lading (B/L) - Express Release or Original Negotiable copies       |
|  [ ] Commercial Invoice - Itemized list of fasteners with exact HS Codes        |
|  [ ] Detailed Packing List - Net/gross weights, box counts, and heat numbers     |
|  [ ] Import Declaration Form (IDF) - Registered via ICUMS before shipping       |
|  [ ] Mill Test Certificates (MTC) - Certifying ISO 898-1 or ASTM mechanical properties|
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

The New GSA EasyPass Portal & Certificate of Conformity (CoC)

Effective July 1, 2026, the Ghana Standards Authority (GSA) assumed direct, exclusive control of the EasyPass PVOC Programme, ending all previous third-party service provider contracts. Under this updated framework, exporters must apply for and obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) directly through the new GSA EasyPass digital portal before shipping. Fasteners fall under restricted mechanical product categories; failing to upload an approved GSA CoC will block your customs clearance and trigger mandatory, costly local testing at Tema Port, alongside severe fines.  
VATupdate
+ 1

Essential Shipping and Customs Documentation

Beyond the GSA certificate, your clearance package must include:

Commercial Invoice: This must show an itemized breakdown of the fastener order. Include separate lines for bolts, nuts, and washers to prevent valuation disputes. It must state the exact unit price, total Free on Board (FOB) value, freight costs, and insurance details.

Packing List: This document must detail the specific contents of each wooden crate or pallet. It should clearly list the total net weight, gross weight, packaging types (e.g., standard 25 kg bags inside outer cartons), and the exact quantity per SKU.

Bill of Lading (B/L): Issued by the ocean carrier, this must match the commercial invoice exactly regarding the consignee's name, Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Ghana Card details, and container seal numbers.

Import Declaration Form (IDF): This is filed electronically through the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS) prior to the ship’s departure from the port of loading.  
Ascope Shipping

Mill Test Certificates (MTC): Crucial for industrial bulk orders, these documents must be supplied by the manufacturer (e.g., TradeGo Fasteners) to verify chemical compositions and mechanical grades, such as Class 8.8, Class 10.9, or ASTM A325 specifications.

sections.title: Customs Clearance Steps for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Ghana Customs utilizes the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS), a unified digital platform that has automated and streamlined cargo processing at Tema Port. Clearing industrial hardware shipments involves three distinct phases.  
Ascope Shipping

       +----------------------------------------------------------+
       | PRE-ARRIVAL PHASE                                        |
       | • Register IDF in ICUMS                                  |
       | • Obtain GSA CoC via EasyPass Portal                     |
       | • Shipping Line uploads Cargo Manifest                   |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | ON-SITE & ASSESSMENT PHASE                               |
       | • Submit entry via Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)     |
       | • ICUMS issues CCVR (Value & Tariff Verification)        |
       | • Pay Duties through Ghana.gov or partner banks           |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | INSPECTION & RELEASE PHASE                               |
       | • Channel Assignment (Green, Yellow, or Red)             |
       | • Physical Inspection / GSA Verification (if Red/Yellow) |
       | • Settle Port/Terminal fees & Gate-Out Cargo             |
       +----------------------------------------------------------+

1. Pre-Arrival Document Submission

The clearance process begins well before the container vessel berths at Tema Port. Once TradeGo Fasteners loads your container and provides the final Bill of Lading, your licensed Customs House Agent (CHA) uploads the commercial invoice, packing list, IDF, and GSA CoC to the ICUMS portal. Simultaneously, the shipping line submits the electronic cargo manifest to the GRA Customs system.  
Guazi
+ 1

ICUMS uses a centralized Home Delivery Value (HDV) database to automatically verify your declared transaction values against global steel averages and historical import records. If your invoice pricing aligns with these market indices, the system smoothly generates a Customs Classification and Valuation Report (CCVR), which specifies the exact duties and taxes owed.  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

2. Physical Cargo Inspection and Channel Routing

Upon the vessel’s arrival and container discharge at the Tema Port terminal, the cargo is routed through one of three ICUMS customs channels:

Green Channel (Direct Release): Paperwork and compliance history are perfect. The cargo is cleared without physical inspection.

Yellow Channel (Document Audit): Discrepancies in HS classifications or missing GSA paperwork trigger an in-depth document review.

Red Channel (Physical Examination): Mandatory for first-time importers, high-volume construction contracts, or shipments flagged with valuation concerns.

During a Red Channel physical examination, customs officers and GSA inspectors verify that the physical fasteners match the declared steel grades, thread types, and dimensions. They will check outer packaging labels for the manufacturer's mark, grade markings (e.g., "8.8" or "A325" stamped on bolt heads), and trace them back to the GSA CoC and Mill Test Certificates.  
Guazi

3. Final Duty Payment and Cargo Release

Once inspection officers clear the shipment, the final step is paying the calculated duties and levies through the Ghana.gov payment portal or authorized partner banks. After payment, the shipping agent issues a Delivery Order (DO), and the Tema Port cargo handling terminal releases the container for transport.  
Ascope Shipping

sections.title: Tariff and Tax Rules for Ghana Fastener Imports at Tema Port
sections.body:
For B2B buyers in Ghana, accurately projecting the Total Landed Cost of fasteners is vital to maintaining project margins. Ghana uses the standardized Harmonized System (HS) Code Chapter 7318 to classify iron and steel fasteners.  
Flexport

Harmonized System (HS) Classifications for Fasteners
HS Code	Product Description	Standard Import Duty
7318.15	Threaded bolts, screws, and studs (with/without nuts/washers)	20%
7318.16	Threaded nuts (hex, lock, flange, heavy hex)	20%
7318.21	Spring washers and other lock washers	20%
7318.22	Non-threaded flat washers	20%
7318.14	Self-tapping screws	20%
Detailed Breakdown of Taxes, Levies, and Fees

Import duties are calculated on the CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) value of the shipment. In addition to the base 20% import duty, several local taxes, statutory levies, and administrative fees are applied during the ICUMS processing phase:  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

Value Added Tax (VAT): 12.5% (calculated on the CIF value plus the import duty).

National Health Insurance Levy (NHIL): 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Education Trust Fund (GETFund) Levy: 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy: 1.0%.

Special Import Levy (SIL): 2.0%.  
Ascope Shipping

ECOWAS Levy: 0.5% (applicable to non-ECOWAS originating cargo).

African Union (AU) Import Levy: 0.2%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Export-Import (EXIM) Bank Levy: 0.75%.  
Ascope Shipping

AfCFTA Preferential Trade Note: Under the rules of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), B2B buyers importing fasteners from other qualified African manufacturing hubs can access significantly reduced or eliminated import tariffs. However, since the bulk of heavy-duty, high-tensile industrial fasteners are still imported from specialized Chinese manufacturers, the standard 20% external tariff and associated levies remain the primary cost structure for most shipments arriving at Tema Port.

Tax Exemptions and Special Incentives

Ghana offers valuable customs exemptions for large-scale industrial projects. Under the Ghana Investment Promotion Centre (GIPC) framework, registered companies executing strategic national infrastructure, mining, or agricultural projects can apply for duty-and-VAT exemptions on bulk imports of industrial fasteners. To benefit from these concessions, the importer must obtain a formal exemption letter from the Ministry of Finance before registering the cargo's IDF in ICUMS.

sections.title: Common Delays and Solutions for Tema Port Fastener Shipment
sections.body:
Even minor errors can stall a Tema port cargo clearance process, transforming an efficient logistics plan into a costly headache. Port demurrage—the daily penalty charged by shipping lines for containers left past their free-time allowance—can quickly erode your profits.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                            TEMA PORT DELAY POINTS & B2B SOLUTIONS                                 |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  1. ICUMS Valuation Flag                                                                          |
|     * Cause: Artificially low invoice values triggered by automatic database checks.              |
|     * Solution: TradeGo Fasteners provides authentic proof of payment and wire transfers.         |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  2. GSA EasyPass Non-Compliance                                                                   |
|     * Cause: Missing or expired Certificate of Conformity (CoC) at arrival.                       |
|     * Solution: Generate the CoC via the GSA digital portal prior to container loading.           |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  3. Weight and Packing Mismatches                                                                 |
|     * Cause: Discrepancies between the packing list and physical container scale weight.          |
|     * Solution: Implement rigorous double-weighing protocols at the factory before sealing.        |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  4. Inconsistent Steel Grade Labeling                                                             |
|     * Cause: Physical markings on fasteners don't match the grades on test certificates.          |
|     * Solution: Standardize laser-etched markings (8.8, 10.9, A325) to align with ISO/ASTM norms. |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

Mitigating ICUMS Valuation Challenges

The GRA’s automated valuation system is highly sensitive to price variations. If your invoice values are lower than the ICUMS benchmark for carbon steel, the system will flag your shipment for undervaluation. To resolve this quickly, always keep a complete "valuation defense package" ready. This should include your signed purchase contract, proof of the advanced bank transfer (such as an escrow receipt or bank swift slip), and a detailed manufacturer price list that explains any volume discounts.  
West Coast Shipping

Navigating Container Handling Fees and Local Port Costs

To protect businesses from unpredictable shipping agent fees, the Ghana Shippers' Authority (GSA) secured a key legal ruling in July 2026. This decision allows the GSA to enforce a strict regulatory cap on local container handling and administrative charges billed by shipping lines. Importers should carefully review local terminal invoices; if a shipping line's local agent charges more than the GSA-mandated ceiling, you can file an immediate complaint with the GSA compliance task force to resolve the billing dispute.  
GBC Ghana Online

sections.title: B2B Fastener Import Best Practices for Tema Port Ghana
sections.body:
Successfully managing imports to Tema Port requires close cooperation between the industrial purchaser, a reliable Chinese manufacturer, and an experienced local logistics partner.

1. Optimize Shipping and Container Loading

Fasteners are high-density, high-weight cargo. A single 20-foot GP container can easily reach its maximum weight limit (typically 26 to 28 metric tons) while remaining only half-full by volume.

Select 20-foot Containers: Always use 20-foot containers rather than 40-foot units for heavy cargo like fasteners. This optimizes ocean freight costs and keeps transport weights within legal limits.

Reinforced Packing: Require your manufacturer to use heavy-duty, moisture-resistant, ISPM-15 certified wooden pallets or crates. Double-strap each pallet to prevent shifting during transit, which can damage packaging and lead to safety rejections during Tema customs inspections.

2. Partner with a Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)

Do not simply hire the cheapest clearing agent. Partner with a reputable, licensed customs broker who has a proven track record of handling heavy metals and industrial hardware. An experienced agent will proactively review your documentation, track your vessel's arrival, and resolve any ICUMS valuation or GSA EasyPass queries within your port's free-time window.  
Guazi

3. Establish a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)

For recurring projects, establish a standard compliance checklist with your Chinese supplier. This ensures that every shipment uses consistent HS codes, matches GSA quality standards, and includes accurate packing lists. This repeatable approach minimizes compliance risks, speeds up your clearances, and keeps your supply chain running smoothly.

sections.title: Mandatory Documents for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Successfully clearing a fastener shipment in Ghana depends entirely on the accuracy and completeness of your shipping dossier. Ghana Customs operates a digital, zero-tolerance documentation system. Discrepancies between your physical cargo and your paperwork will trigger immediate audits, leading to severe storage penalties.

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                         MANDATORY DOCUMENT CHECKLIST                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [ ] Certificate of Conformity (CoC) - Issued via new GSA EasyPass Portal       |
|  [ ] Bill of Lading (B/L) - Express Release or Original Negotiable copies       |
|  [ ] Commercial Invoice - Itemized list of fasteners with exact HS Codes        |
|  [ ] Detailed Packing List - Net/gross weights, box counts, and heat numbers     |
|  [ ] Import Declaration Form (IDF) - Registered via ICUMS before shipping       |
|  [ ] Mill Test Certificates (MTC) - Certifying ISO 898-1 or ASTM mechanical properties|
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------------+

The New GSA EasyPass Portal & Certificate of Conformity (CoC)

Effective July 1, 2026, the Ghana Standards Authority (GSA) assumed direct, exclusive control of the EasyPass PVOC Programme, ending all previous third-party service provider contracts. Under this updated framework, exporters must apply for and obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) directly through the new GSA EasyPass digital portal before shipping. Fasteners fall under restricted mechanical product categories; failing to upload an approved GSA CoC will block your customs clearance and trigger mandatory, costly local testing at Tema Port, alongside severe fines.  
VATupdate
+ 1

Essential Shipping and Customs Documentation

Beyond the GSA certificate, your clearance package must include:

Commercial Invoice: This must show an itemized breakdown of the fastener order. Include separate lines for bolts, nuts, and washers to prevent valuation disputes. It must state the exact unit price, total Free on Board (FOB) value, freight costs, and insurance details.

Packing List: This document must detail the specific contents of each wooden crate or pallet. It should clearly list the total net weight, gross weight, packaging types (e.g., standard 25 kg bags inside outer cartons), and the exact quantity per SKU.

Bill of Lading (B/L): Issued by the ocean carrier, this must match the commercial invoice exactly regarding the consignee's name, Tax Identification Number (TIN) or Ghana Card details, and container seal numbers.

Import Declaration Form (IDF): This is filed electronically through the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS) prior to the ship’s departure from the port of loading.  
Ascope Shipping

Mill Test Certificates (MTC): Crucial for industrial bulk orders, these documents must be supplied by the manufacturer (e.g., TradeGo Fasteners) to verify chemical compositions and mechanical grades, such as Class 8.8, Class 10.9, or ASTM A325 specifications.

sections.title: Customs Clearance Steps for Tema Port Fastener Import
sections.body:
Ghana Customs utilizes the Integrated Customs Management System (ICUMS), a unified digital platform that has automated and streamlined cargo processing at Tema Port. Clearing industrial hardware shipments involves three distinct phases.  
Ascope Shipping

       +----------------------------------------------------------+
       | PRE-ARRIVAL PHASE                                        |
       | • Register IDF in ICUMS                                  |
       | • Obtain GSA CoC via EasyPass Portal                     |
       | • Shipping Line uploads Cargo Manifest                   |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | ON-SITE & ASSESSMENT PHASE                               |
       | • Submit entry via Licensed Customs House Agent (CHA)     |
       | • ICUMS issues CCVR (Value & Tariff Verification)        |
       | • Pay Duties through Ghana.gov or partner banks           |
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
                                    |
                                    v
       +----------------------------+-----------------------------+
       | INSPECTION & RELEASE PHASE                               |
       | • Channel Assignment (Green, Yellow, or Red)             |
       | • Physical Inspection / GSA Verification (if Red/Yellow) |
       | • Settle Port/Terminal fees & Gate-Out Cargo             |
       +----------------------------------------------------------+

1. Pre-Arrival Document Submission

The clearance process begins well before the container vessel berths at Tema Port. Once TradeGo Fasteners loads your container and provides the final Bill of Lading, your licensed Customs House Agent (CHA) uploads the commercial invoice, packing list, IDF, and GSA CoC to the ICUMS portal. Simultaneously, the shipping line submits the electronic cargo manifest to the GRA Customs system.  
Guazi
+ 1

ICUMS uses a centralized Home Delivery Value (HDV) database to automatically verify your declared transaction values against global steel averages and historical import records. If your invoice pricing aligns with these market indices, the system smoothly generates a Customs Classification and Valuation Report (CCVR), which specifies the exact duties and taxes owed.  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

2. Physical Cargo Inspection and Channel Routing

Upon the vessel’s arrival and container discharge at the Tema Port terminal, the cargo is routed through one of three ICUMS customs channels:

Green Channel (Direct Release): Paperwork and compliance history are perfect. The cargo is cleared without physical inspection.

Yellow Channel (Document Audit): Discrepancies in HS classifications or missing GSA paperwork trigger an in-depth document review.

Red Channel (Physical Examination): Mandatory for first-time importers, high-volume construction contracts, or shipments flagged with valuation concerns.

During a Red Channel physical examination, customs officers and GSA inspectors verify that the physical fasteners match the declared steel grades, thread types, and dimensions. They will check outer packaging labels for the manufacturer's mark, grade markings (e.g., "8.8" or "A325" stamped on bolt heads), and trace them back to the GSA CoC and Mill Test Certificates.  
Guazi

3. Final Duty Payment and Cargo Release

Once inspection officers clear the shipment, the final step is paying the calculated duties and levies through the Ghana.gov payment portal or authorized partner banks. After payment, the shipping agent issues a Delivery Order (DO), and the Tema Port cargo handling terminal releases the container for transport.  
Ascope Shipping

sections.title: Tariff and Tax Rules for Ghana Fastener Imports at Tema Port
sections.body:
For B2B buyers in Ghana, accurately projecting the Total Landed Cost of fasteners is vital to maintaining project margins. Ghana uses the standardized Harmonized System (HS) Code Chapter 7318 to classify iron and steel fasteners.  
Flexport

Harmonized System (HS) Classifications for Fasteners
HS Code	Product Description	Standard Import Duty
7318.15	Threaded bolts, screws, and studs (with/without nuts/washers)	20%
7318.16	Threaded nuts (hex, lock, flange, heavy hex)	20%
7318.21	Spring washers and other lock washers	20%
7318.22	Non-threaded flat washers	20%
7318.14	Self-tapping screws	20%
Detailed Breakdown of Taxes, Levies, and Fees

Import duties are calculated on the CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) value of the shipment. In addition to the base 20% import duty, several local taxes, statutory levies, and administrative fees are applied during the ICUMS processing phase:  
Ascope Shipping
+ 1

Value Added Tax (VAT): 12.5% (calculated on the CIF value plus the import duty).

National Health Insurance Levy (NHIL): 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Education Trust Fund (GETFund) Levy: 2.5%.  
Ascope Shipping

COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy: 1.0%.

Special Import Levy (SIL): 2.0%.  
Ascope Shipping

ECOWAS Levy: 0.5% (applicable to non-ECOWAS originating cargo).

African Union (AU) Import Levy: 0.2%.  
Ascope Shipping

Ghana Export-Import (EXIM) Bank Levy: 0.75%.  
Ascope Shipping

AfCFTA Preferential Trade Note: Under the rules of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), B2B buyers importing fasteners from other qualified African manufacturing hubs can access significantly reduced or eliminated import tariffs. However, since the bulk of heavy-duty, high-tensile industrial fasteners are still imported from specialized Chinese manufacturers, the standard 20% external tariff and associated levies remain the primary cost structure for most shipments arriving at Tema Port.

Tax Exemptions and Special Incentives

Ghana offers valuable customs exemptions for large-scale industrial projects. Under the Ghana Investment Promotion Centre (GIPC) framework, registered companies executing strategic national infrastructure, mining, or agricultural projects can apply for duty-and-VAT exemptions on bulk imports of industrial fasteners. To benefit from these concessions, the importer must obtain a formal exemption letter from the Ministry of Finance before registering the cargo's IDF in ICUMS.
